title: leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. cantabricum
intro: Leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. cantabricum, also known as the Cantabrian oxeye daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. cantabricum include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
